design_philosophy: Cream foundation, soft pink as emotion, gold as premium cue. The UI should feel like a premium wedding magazine. 'Quiet luxury' layout—clear white space, cream blocks, and gold accents just enough to signal premium without being flashy.

components.card: Clean white backgrounds (#FFFFFF) on cream canvas (#FDFBF7), soft shadow, no hard borders. Rounded-3xl.
components.primary_button: Blush pink (#F4C2C2) or Gold (#D4AF37) background, rounded-full, px-8 py-4, centered bold text.
components.secondary_button: Transparent background, Gold (#D4AF37) border, rounded-full, px-8 py-4.
components.date_picker_grid: 7 columns. Available dates: white background, subtle border. Unavailable dates: light pink/gray background, crossed out or muted text. Selected date: Gold background, white text.
components.inputs: Floating label style or clean labels above. Cream/white background, thin border on focus, rounded-xl px-4 py-3.

motion.transitions: Gentle scale-in and fade. Use LayoutAnimation in React Native or standard React Navigation transitions (slide from right).
motion.loading: Soft pulsing opacity on skeleton loaders or a sleek gold spinner.

vendor_venue.usage: Hero image or Venue vendor thumbnail.

media.image_guidance: Images should have a max-height of 400px on detail screens. Use resizeMode='cover' for absolute positioning with bottom linear gradients to ensure text overlay readability.

architecture_and_implementation.stack: React Native (Expo), NativeWind (Tailwind CSS), TypeScript, React Navigation.

structure.app/: Contains expo-router file based routing or standard navigation setup.
structure.mock/data.ts: Contains fallback mock data for vendors and dates.

architecture_and_implementation.testing_attributes: All interactive elements must include a testID attribute (equivalent to data-testid in web) formatted in kebab-case (e.g., testID='book-now-button').

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Initialize layout with a clean navigation wrapper using React Navigation.
2. Implement `HomeScreen` with a vertical ScrollView of vendor cards. Use provided image URLs.
3. Implement `VendorDetailScreen` displaying full image, description, and 'Check Availability' button.
4. Implement `BookingScreen`. MUST include a custom date grid (7 columns) simulating a calendar month.
5. For the date grid: Fetch booked dates via `availability.ts` (use setTimeout mock initially). Mark returned dates as unavailable (visually distinct and non-clickable).
6. Build a simple form: Name, Phone, Package selection. Disable submit until all fields + date are selected.
7. On submit, call `webhook.ts` to POST data. Show a loading overlay, then navigate to `SuccessScreen`.
8. Ensure styling uses NativeWind classes mapping to the provided color palette (e.g., bg-[#FDFBF7], text-[#221E1C], bg-[#F4C2C2]).
9. Use Lucide React Native or similar for elegant line icons (chevron-left, calendar, check-circle).
10. Remember to apply safe area views and generous padding to maintain the 'premium' airy feel.
